guest: Jamie Siminoff, Founder of Ring Doorbell (sold to Amazon for $1.15B)

business_ideas.idea: Smart video doorbell solving inability to hear doorbell in garage
Solar-powered bug elimination system for farms (current obsession)
Multi-camera home security system and neighborhood safety network

predictions.text: Global bug control market hides 5-10B dollar opportunity despite current solutions
Neighborhood safety and crime prevention will be primary driver of Ring expansion

dynamics: Jamie drives with inventor's mindset and operational depth. Sam probes founder psychology, resilience through crises. High rapport as Jamie shares ADT lawsuit crisis and wire-fund celebration moment.
frameworks: Problem-first not technology-first ideation (start with unsolved problem, then apply tech)
Pre-awareness marketing (modify existing category, don't educate new ones)
Snowball accumulation (roll small ideas into bigger company through attention to detail)
learnings: Founder desperation (cutting salary to $150K on $480M revenue business) creates focus that succeeds
Extreme detail obsession (tile interior of product, 11-second batter timing precision) compounds over time
Resilience through multi-crisis (ADT lawsuit, Amazon pullout, capital crunch same December week)

banger_quotes.quote: I just don't know how to stop and sometimes it's not smart
The insane part of insanely great is what actually matters

numbers_dropped: Ring grew to $4B annual revenue post-Amazon (10x)
Raised at $150K personal salary despite $480M company revenue
Wire hit April 2018 during conference speech
First house bought age 22 for $47K in Pittsburgh
notable: Jamie talks candidly about desperation (making $150K while company did $480M), life in Missouri farm post-acquisition, investing in towns, and staying on at Amazon 7 years post-sale.
